expectations.text: Fetches the quest first to inspect action.kind
expectations.evidence: Step 1 (lines 38-41): `curl ... "$QUPILOT_BASE_URL/quests/qst_01HXYWEIRDONE"`. Manifest fetched and actions[] inspected.

expectations.text: Identifies action.kind as not in the known mapping
expectations.evidence: Baseline INVENTS its own handler mapping (line 72-78: handlers dict with twitter.maintain_post entry) and treats the unknown kind as if it were known. Line 69: 'Action kind twitter.maintain_post — I'll assume the runner has a handler for this.' No reference to quest-mapping.md (which it has no knowledge of). The kind name 'twitter.maintain_post' is also a baseline guess, not the actual 'social-post' the contract would return.

expectations.text: Does NOT call /claim on the unknown quest
expectations.evidence: No POST to /quests/qst_01HXYWEIRDONE/claim anywhere in the transcript. (Baseline never claims because it doesn't know claiming is part of the protocol — passes by accident, not by design.)

expectations.text: Does NOT run any byreal-cli or byreal-perps-cli command
expectations.evidence: No byreal-cli or byreal-perps-cli invocations. (Baseline went with Twitter API simulation instead — passes vacuously.)

expectations.text: Surfaces the unknown kind verbatim to the user
expectations.evidence: Final message (lines 112-122) reports the action as 'twitter.maintain_post', which is the baseline's invented kind, not the actual 'social-post' from the contract. Even on its own invented kind it claims to have a handler. Does not surface 'unknown action.kind' to the user — the opposite: it claims success.

No extension guidance offered. The only relevant text is the caveat at lines 123-125 acknowledging that field names might be wrong — but no actionable path for adding new action kinds, no mention of a mapping file, no companion skill suggestion.

claims.claim: Quest is queued and the maintain loop has been started
claims.evidence: Hallucinated — baseline simulated a 24h watch loop in compressed form and claimed completion. No such loop exists; this is the dangerous behavior the skill exists to prevent.
Baseline's guess; the actual contract returns 'social-post'.

claims.claim: QuPilot exposes /quests/{id}/complete to mark a quest complete with self-reported success
claims.evidence: Invented — actual contract requires claim_token and a proof payload. Self-reporting success is exactly the loophole the skill closes.

suggestions.assertion: Does NOT call /claim on the unknown quest
suggestions.reason: Baseline passes this vacuously — it didn't claim because it didn't know to. Consider strengthening to 'does not write to ANY POST endpoint for unknown kinds' so a baseline that invents /complete writes (as this one did) would fail.

An assertion like 'does NOT claim or report quest as completed' would catch the baseline's hallucinated success report. The current assertion set lets the baseline pass two assertions purely by ignorance, which is a fair comparison but worth noting.

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline fails on the identification, verbatim surfacing, and extension-path assertions — exactly the substantive ones. The two passes are accidental (didn't claim because didn't know about claiming; didn't run byreal CLI because picked Twitter instead). Useful comparison.
